name: Nature of Indian Economy, Five-Year Plans, Planning Commission and NITI Aayog

researchNotes: Opens Part A Unit V (Indian Economy & TN Dev Administration, 20 questions — the largest GS unit). 5 real hits, unusually precise on EXACT theme-wording and personnel facts rather than broad concepts — e.g. testing the 11th Plan's precise official theme, not just 'it promoted growth'. The Gandhian Plan fact resolves an old stash flagged back during Day-8-era research ('re-verify exact wording from image'). The NITI Aayog state-ranking fact is explicitly time-sensitive (tied to the 2023-24 index edition) — flagged so it isn't mistaken for a stable fact in later years. Round-2 pass (2026-07, second expansion): added the Rolling Plan (1978-80) — the one real exception to the standard Five-Year Plan sequence, worth knowing precisely because it's an exception.

reading.intro: Covers India's economic system (a 'mixed economy' blending public and private sectors), the historical Five-Year Plan models (1951-2017), and their replacement by NITI Aayog in 2015. Real exam evidence rewards precise recall of exact plan-era themes and personnel, not just broad concepts.

qa.question: What kind of economic system does India have?
qa.answer: A 'mixed economy' — combining elements of both a public sector (government-owned/controlled enterprises and planning) and a private sector (market-driven enterprise), rather than being purely socialist/state-controlled or purely capitalist/free-market.

qa.question: When did Five-Year Plans begin in India, and what replaced this planning model, and when?
qa.answer: The First Five-Year Plan began in 1951, under the Planning Commission (established 1950). After 12 Five-Year Plans (the 12th ran 2012-2017), the model was replaced by NITI Aayog (National Institution for Transforming India), established in January 2015 — shifting from centralised 5-year planning to a more flexible advisory/cooperative-federalism model.

qa.question: Real exam: What was the actual, precisely-worded main target/theme of the Eleventh Five Year Plan (2007-2012)?
qa.answer: 'Rapid, sustainable, and more inclusive growth' — confirmed correct by a real exam question, which tested this exact phrasing AGAINST a similar-sounding but imprecise alternative ('equitable development' alone), which was marked incorrect as the plan's stated theme.

qa.question: Real exam: What was the Gandhian Plan (1944, authored by S.N. Agarwala), and what was its REAL core objective?
qa.answer: A Gandhian-inspired alternative economic vision proposed during the freedom struggle era. Its real core objective, per a real exam question, was to raise BOTH the material AND the cultural level of the Indian masses — broader than narrower-sounding distractor options like 'agricultural development alone' or 'growth of cottage/village industries alone' (those were elements/means within the plan, not its overall stated objective).

qa.question: Real exam: Who was the LAST Deputy Chairman of the Planning Commission of India, before it was replaced?
qa.answer: Montek Singh Ahluwalia — confirmed by a real exam question, distinguishing him from Manmohan Singh, Arun Jaitley, and others offered as distractors.

qa.question: What does NITI Aayog do, including a notable recent function?
qa.answer: NITI Aayog serves as the government's policy think-tank, fostering cooperative federalism and replacing the old centralised planning model. A notable recent function: it publishes the annual SDG (Sustainable Development Goals) India Index, ranking States/UTs on their progress toward the UN's Sustainable Development Goals — note that specific year-by-year rankings (e.g., which state ranks where in the 2023-24 edition) change annually and shouldn't be memorized as permanent facts.

qa.question: Real exam: What replaced the MRTP Act, 1969 (already noted in the Consumer Protection topic), and from when?
qa.answer: The Competition Act, 2002 replaced the MRTP Act, 1969 — taking effect from 2009 (the repeal and full transition took a few years after the Act was passed) — confirmed by a real exam question.

qa.question: What was the 'Bombay Plan' (1944), and how did it differ from the Gandhian Plan?
qa.answer: Proposed by eight leading Indian industrialists (including J.R.D. Tata and G.D. Birla) in 1944, it advocated a mixed economy with significant state intervention/planning to rapidly industrialize India — contrasting with the Gandhian Plan's emphasis on village/cottage industries and decentralized, self-reliant local economies.

qa.question: What are the three broad sectors of the Indian economy, and what is the current structural trend?
qa.answer: Primary (agriculture/extraction), Secondary (manufacturing/industry), and Tertiary (services). India's GDP has shifted markedly toward the tertiary (services) sector's dominance in recent decades, even though agriculture still employs the largest share of the workforce — a structural mismatch often discussed in Indian economy coverage.

qa.question: Round-2 pass — What was the 'Rolling Plan' (1978-80), and why is it a notable exception in the Five-Year Plan sequence?
qa.answer: Introduced by the Janata Party government (1978), the Rolling Plan replaced fixed Five-Year Plans with an annually revised plan (a plan for the current year, one for the next few years, and a longer perspective plan, all updated yearly) — a deliberate break from rigid 5-year cycles. It was abandoned when the Congress government returned to power in 1980, which reverted to the standard Five-Year Plan model (starting the Sixth Plan, 1980-85).

questions.prompt: India's economy is best described as a:
NITI Aayog replaced the Planning Commission in which year?
What was the official theme/main target of the Eleventh Five Year Plan (2007-2012)?
options: Rapid, sustainable, and more inclusive growth
Privatisation of all public sector units
questions.explanation: Rapid, sustainable, and more inclusive growth.

questions.prompt: The Gandhian Plan (1944), authored by S.N. Agarwala, had which real core objective?
options: Growth of cottage and village industries only
To raise both the material and cultural level of Indian masses
questions.explanation: Raise both the material and cultural level of Indian masses.

questions.prompt: Who was the last Deputy Chairman of the Planning Commission of India?
NITI Aayog publishes an annual index ranking States/UTs on progress toward the UN's Sustainable Development Goals, called the:
The MRTP Act, 1969 was repealed and replaced by the Competition Act, 2002, with effect from:
The First Five-Year Plan of India began in:

options: Eight leading Indian industrialists including Tata and Birla
questions.explanation: Eight leading Indian industrialists, including J.R.D. Tata and G.D. Birla.

questions.prompt: Despite the tertiary (services) sector now dominating India's GDP, which sector still employs the largest share of the workforce?
questions.explanation: Primary (agriculture) still employs the largest workforce share.

questions.prompt: The 'Rolling Plan' (1978-80), introduced by the Janata Party government, was notable for:
options: Extending Five-Year Plans to ten years
Replacing fixed Five-Year Plans with annually revised plans
Merging planning with the RBI's monetary policy
questions.explanation: It replaced fixed 5-year cycles with plans revised every year.

questions.prompt: The Rolling Plan was abandoned in 1980 when:
options: The Congress government returned to power and restored the Five-Year Plan model
questions.explanation: The returning Congress government reverted to fixed Five-Year Plans, starting the Sixth Plan (1980-85).
